cards.meaning: once; as soon as; in the event that (connective)
cards.example: 一旦停火破裂，油价就会马上反弹。
cards.examplePinyin: yídàn tínghuǒ pòliè, yóujià jiù huì mǎshàng fǎntán
cards.exampleMeaning: Once the ceasefire breaks down, oil prices will rebound immediately.
cards.note: HSK4 advanced connective, 书面语/formal. Stronger than 如果 (rúguǒ, 'if') — it stresses 'the very moment it happens,' so it pairs with a following 就/会 clause stating the consequence. News and finance desks lean on it (一旦…就…). Citation tone of 一 is 1st, but before the 4th-tone 旦 it sandhis to 2nd: yídàn.

cards.example: 人工智能投资有很大的金融风险。
cards.examplePinyin: réngōng zhìnéng tóuzī yǒu hěn dà de jīnróng fēngxiǎn
cards.exampleMeaning: AI investment carries great financial risk.
cards.note: HSK4 abstract noun. Contrast 危险 (wēixiǎn) = concrete, physical danger (crossing a road) vs 风险 (fēngxiǎn) = abstract, measurable risk — finance almost always uses 风险. Common collocations: 金融风险 (financial risk), 系统性风险 (systemic risk), 防范风险 (guard against risk).
cards.tiesTo: BIS warning on AI-boom financial-stability risk

cards.meaning: apprehension; an anxious foreboding about what may come
cards.example: May pangamba ang mga mamumuhunan na baka pumutok ang AI bubble.
cards.exampleMeaning: Investors feel apprehension that the AI bubble might burst.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: distinct from 'takot,' which is immediate, raw fear of something in front of you. 'Pangamba' is anticipatory, forward-looking unease — slightly literary, the right word for market anxiety about a risk that hasn't arrived yet.

cards.meaning: the utmost; the extreme; the absolute limit
cards.example: Nasa sukdulang antas ang ginto, at ang utang sa margin ay nasa sukdulan din.
cards.exampleMeaning: Gold is at an extreme level, and margin debt is also at its limit.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: stronger than 'matindi' (intense) — 'sukdulan' marks the very edge, the maximum point. Common pairings: sukdulang panganib (extreme danger), sukdulang tuwa (utmost joy), sukdulang antas (extreme level).
